news.headline: Chesapeake Utilities Corp's Ohio subsidiary, Aspire Energy, celebrates 10 years & seeks growth opportunities after merger in 2015.
news.summary: Chesapeake Utilities Corporation (NYSE: CPK) marked a significant milestone by commemorating the 10-year anniversary of its Ohio subsidiary based in Dover, Del. On April 1, 2015, the Company finalized the merger of Gatherco Inc. into Aspire Energy of Ohio LLC. This merger allows the company to provide energy services through natural gas.

news.headline: Vantage Capital Advisors sells 556-unit Midwest multifamily portfolio for $95.5M after strategic success.
news.summary: In Lincoln and Bellevue, Nebraska, on April 2, 2025, Vantage Capital Advisors, a prominent player in multifamily real estate investment, shared that they have sold a dual-property multifamily portfolio encompassing 556 units for a significant $95.5 million. The properties were obtained earlier on in the year.

news.headline: Invesco Fund Manager bets against USD before Trump's tariffs, as reported by Bloomberg.
news.summary: In the latest update, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has decreased to 4.8%, marking a significant improvement from the previous month. Additionally, the economy has added over 250,000 new jobs in various sectors, indicating a positive trend towards recovery. The stock market has also witnessed a substantial increase, with the main index rising by 2.5% in the last week. Overall, these numbers reflect a promising outlook for the economy as it continues to bounce back from the challenges posed by the pandemic.

news.headline: Inventor from Jamestown, R.I. creates LIQUID CAPS to preserve leftover paint in paint cans, preventing premature drying.
news.summary: A resident of Jamestown, Rhode Island, recently unveiled a new invention called LIQUID CAPS with the goal of preserving leftover paint in cans. The inventor shared that the innovative design aims to prevent premature drying, aging, and thickening of usable paint. This development was introduced in Pittsburgh on April 2, 2025, with the inventor expressing the desire to address the common issue of maintaining the integrity of leftover paint. This invention could potentially revolutionize the way people store and reuse paint, offering practical solutions for approximately 1000 characters.

news.headline: Industrial robotics market to reach $37.1 billion by 2031, growing at 6.8% CAGR due to automation demand and AI advancements.
news.summary: Rising demand for automation and advancements in AI-powered robotics are fueling market growth in Redding, California. A recent market research report on the Industrial Robotics Market indicates significant traction. The report covers size, share, forecasts, and trends analysis by component, including robots and robot-related technologies. The data, collected up to April 2, 2025, showcases promising growth prospects, driven by a surge in demand for automated solutions. This shift towards robotics is indicative of the industry's growing reliance on AI technologies for operational efficiency and enhanced productivity.

news.headline: YesCare to provide opioid treatment in Louisville Metro Dept of Corrections, first in KY with all FDA-approved medications under one license.
news.summary: In Louisville, Kentucky, YesCare has announced plans to establish the state's inaugural jail-based opioid treatment program. This initiative will provide access to all FDA-approved medications for opioid use disorder under a single license.

news.headline: Bitfarms secures $300M loan from Macquarie for Panther Creek project in PA with 500MW capacity; miners focusing on AI/HPC.
news.summary: Bitfarms has secured a $300 million loan from Macquarie Group to support the development of its high-performance computing (HPC) data centers. The loan includes an initial $50 million for the Panther Creek data center project in Pennsylvania, with the remaining $250 million to be released upon achieving development milestones. Panther Creek will have a 500-megawatt capacity and is expected to be sought after by HPC tenants. Despite facing regulatory challenges in expanding its energy capacity, Bitfarms acquired Stronghold Digital Mining for additional capacity. Bitfarms mined 654 Bitcoin in the final quarter of 2024 at an average cost of $60,800 and currently holds 1,152 BTC. Industry pressure has led miners, including Bitfarms, to adopt a long-term Bitcoin "hodl" strategy. Other companies like Hive Digital, Core Scientific, Hut8, and Bit Digital are also pivoting towards AI and HPC, with AI applications showing potential for higher revenue compared to crypto mining. Hive Digital, for example, has repurposed Nvidia GPUs for AI tasks generating $2.00 per hour in revenue. MARA Holdings, on the other hand, holds 46,374 BTC and plans a $2 billion stock offering to acquire more Bitcoin.

news.headline: QualiZeal named Tricentis' AMS Rookie Partner of the Year in Irving, Texas.
news.summary: QualiZeal, a company based in Irving, Texas, was recently named the AMS Rookie Partner of the Year by Tricentis, a notable achievement in the field of AI technology. This recognition demonstrates QualiZeal's impressive performance and dedication in this partnership. The award highlights their outstanding contributions and success in this collaborative endeavor. This acknowledgment solidifies QualiZeal's position as a promising player in the industry.

news.headline: Global LFP Battery Recycling Market 2025-2035 report shows emerging economies driving growth with increasing EVs and energy storage usage.
news.summary: In Dublin, a report titled "LFP Battery Recycling Market - A Global and Regional Analysis: Focus on Application, Product, and Country Level Analysis - Analysis and Forecast, 2025-2035" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The Global LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate) Battery Recycling Market is experiencing significant growth attributed to the rising utilization of LFP batteries in electric vehicles (EVs), energy storage systems, and industrial sectors. LFP batteries are preferred for their safety features. This growth trend is projected to continue between 2025 and 2035.

news.headline: Touchstone Climbing in Berkeley, California transitions to a Public Benefit Corporation, prioritizing social and environmental responsibility.
news.summary: Touchstone Climbing, based in Berkeley, California, declared on April 2, 2025, via PRNewswire, that it has achieved the status of a Public Benefit Corporation (PBC). This move represents a significant step in their dedication to integrating social and environmental responsibilities into their business operations. Touchstone Climbing is notable for being the first organization in their sector to make this transition.

news.headline: Tesla delivered 336,681 EVs, below expectations and lowest in years. Stock initially fell but later rebounded.
news.summary: In the first quarter, Tesla delivered a total of 336,681 electric vehicles, which fell below expectations and marked the lowest quarterly performance in over two years. This disappointing result led to a decline in Tesla's stock. However, the stock later reversed its downward trend and started to rise again.

news.headline: Nintendo announces new Switch 2 game console with custom Nvidia processor, launching June 5 for $449.99.
news.summary: Nintendo has announced the release of the Switch 2 game console, equipped with a custom Nvidia processor. The highly anticipated device is scheduled to hit the market on June 5th at a price point of $449.99. This news was shared by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Hims' stock drops as expectations for branded tirzepatide lower; faces unexpected hitch in obesity plan.
news.summary: On Wednesday, Hims stock saw a decline after analysts expressed caution regarding the prospects of Him & Hers' branded tirzepatide. Shares of the company decreased following concerns over the efficacy of its obesity treatment plan that is similar to Lilly's product. The unexpected hitch in the development of the drug has led to a decline in investor confidence. As a result, Hims stock faced a significant reversal, reflecting the uncertainties surrounding the branded tirzepatide. This setback comes amid growing competition in the pharmaceutical market.

news.headline: Palantir set to secure U.S. Army contract for annual recurring revenue close to $100 million, predicts analyst.
news.summary: A William Blair analyst predicts that Palantir is on the verge of securing a U.S. Army contract that has the potential to generate nearly $100 million in annual recurring revenue. This contract is expected to involve collaborations with L3Harris and Anduril. The analyst's insights were recently shared, foreseeing a significant financial boost for Palantir through this agreement with the U.S. Army.

news.headline: CoreWeave stock volatile post downsized IPO, 40% rally Tuesday. Shares trending higher, could stay volatile.
news.summary: In Wednesday morning trading, shares of CoreWeave experienced fluctuations but were trending upwards in recent activity, gaining further momentum after a 40% surge the previous day. The rally continued following a downsized IPO, suggesting potential for continued volatility in the stock.

news.headline: New Mario Kart and Donkey Kong Bananza part of Nintendo Switch 2's early game lineup, releasing in June with technical upgrades.
news.summary: Mario Kart World and Donkey Kong Bananza are among the games announced as part of the early lineup for the upcoming console. The company also revealed information about the console’s technical enhancements.

news.headline: ArtPrize 2025 registration opens with $600,000 prizes. Event in Grand Rapids draws 800K visitors.
news.summary: ArtPrize 2025 has commenced artist registration in Grand Rapids, Michigan, attracting massive crowds to the renowned art competition. With close to 800,000 visitors yearly, ArtPrize converts the city into a sprawling gallery, offering an interactive display of artistic talent.

news.headline: International Paper surpasses goal, conserves 1M acres of forestland, a step towards sustainable packaging solutions.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, in Memphis, Tennessee, International Paper disclosed that it has surpassed its sustainability objective by conserving and restoring over 1 million acres of crucial forestland. Known as the forefront provider of sustainable packaging solutions, the company's achievement highlights its commitment to ecological preservation. International Paper's stocks are listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol IP and LSE under IPC.

news.headline: Avisi Technologies reports positive interim data for VisiPlate® Aqueous Shunt at the American Glaucoma Society.
news.summary: In Redwood City, California on April 2, 2025, Avisi Technologies, a company specializing in ophthalmic devices in the clinical stage, shared positive interim findings after six months from the VITA trial assessing the VisiPlate® device. This innovative product is an ultrathin aqueous shunt with multiple channels.

news.headline: 46% of Americans plan to dig without notifying 811, risking disruptions - PA One Call. #UnsafeDigging #UtilityDamage
news.summary: In West Mifflin, Pennsylvania on April 2, 2025, it was highlighted that April is a crucial period for ensuring safety during digging projects. The importance of contacting 811 for every project was emphasized to safeguard workers and prevent expensive utility damage. As part of National Safe Digging Month, Pennsylvania One Call System, Inc., also known as PA One, promoted this safety measure. It serves as a reminder for individuals to follow safety protocols, avoid accidents, and protect utility infrastructure.

news.headline: Sheikha Al Nowais emphasizes private sector leadership at IHIF 2025 in Berlin. UNWTO Secretary-General Candidate discusses execution and regional investment.
news.summary: Candidate Sheikha Al Nowais highlighted three key priorities - execution, partnership, and regional investment, during her address at the International Hospitality Investment Forum in Berlin on April 2, 2025. She underlined the importance of these factors in her bid for the position of Secretary-General of the UN World Tourism Organization. The event, IHIF, witnessed her advocating for a strategic approach to drive the organization forward. The emphasis on execution, partnership, and regional investment reflects her vision for the role, focusing on tangible actions and collaborations, particularly at a regional level. This indicates a strong commitment to driving impactful change within the organization.

news.headline: Zum named to FT's 2025 Fastest Growing Companies List for third year in a row. Based in California.
news.summary: For the third consecutive year, Zūm has achieved a spot on the esteemed list of "The Americas' Fastest Growing Companies" by the Financial Times. This recognition, announced on April 2, 2025, underscores Zūm's success in the region. This acknowledgment holds significance for the company as it signifies its continued growth and impact.

news.headline: Inventor from Los Angeles creates WILD HAIRS tweezer grooming kit for cars, with storage for tweezers and mirror.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 2, 2025, an inventor from Los Angeles, California shared their inspiration for creating the WILD HAIRS product. The inventor explained that they identified a need for a convenient storage solution for tweezers and a mirror in cars, particularly for quick touch-ups like removing stray facial hairs. The WILD HAIRS innovation aims to address this need and enhance on-the-go grooming.

news.headline: Long Island Aquarium celebrates 25th anniversary with otter pups, penguin egg hunts. Sponsored by Bank of America.
news.summary: In Riverhead, New York, the Long Island Aquarium is experiencing a season of festivities this spring. They are commemorating their 25th anniversary, with the support of Bank of America as the sponsor. Alongside this milestone, the aquarium is also introducing new life and reviving beloved visitor activities. This year's celebrations are particularly special as it marks a quarter-century since the establishment of the aquarium.

news.headline: Global Regenerative Thermal Oxidizer Market expected to reach USD 19.43 billion by 2034 with a 4.28% CAGR. Key players investing in R&D and AI integration for efficiency and compliance. Asia-Pacific market growing rapidly due to stricter environmental regulations. Triple bed RTOs dominate market due to efficiency, while double bed RTOs popular for medium-sized applications. Market players collaborating and innovating to enhance emission reduction solutions.
news.summary: The global Regenerative Thermal Oxidizer (RTO) market is projected to grow significantly, with a value of around USD 19.43 Billion by 2034 from approximately USD 12.74 Billion in 2024, showing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of about 4.28% during the forecast period 2025-2034. The market is driven by increased demand for eco-friendly air pollution control technologies, with key players focusing on smart monitoring systems that incorporate Internet of Things (IoT) and artificial intelligence (AI) for real-time monitoring. The main challenges include high installation costs, maintenance and the need for skilled operators. Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing market region, particularly led by China and India's initiatives to reduce industrial emissions. The report highlights the dominance of triple bed RTOs due to efficiency in VOC destruction and heat recovery, as well as the increasing popularity of hybrid RTO solutions combining catalytic and thermal oxidation technologies. Key market players are expected to invest in research & development and collaborate on innovative emission reduction solutions to further develop the market landscape.

news.headline: ExxonMobil announces leadership changes: Karen McKee to retire on May 1, 2025; Matt Crocker appointed as successor.
news.summary: In Spring, Texas, ExxonMobil (NYSE: XOM) has revealed that Karen T. McKee, the current president of ExxonMobil Product Solutions Company and vice president of Exxon Mobil Corporation, is set to retire on May 1, 2025. Taking her place will be Matt Crocker, who has been appointed as the new president of ExxonMobil Product Solutions Company and vice president of Exxon Mobil Corporation. The transition in leadership is scheduled to take effect on May 1, 2025. Darren Woods, the Chief Executive Officer, expressed gratitude towards McKee for her dedicated service to the company.

news.headline: Tractor Supply Co. ready for spring with month-long special events promoting outdoor activities; Chief Marketing Officer excited.
news.summary: BRENTWOOD, Tenn.-- Tractor Supply Company, the leading rural lifestyle retailer in the U.S., is launching spring season festivities throughout the month with various special events. The Chief Marketing Officer, Kimberley Gardiner, expressed excitement over the increased activity at Tractor Supply as customers are eager to engage in outdoor projects. The company is facilitating an easier way to commence spring tasks and embrace the essence of "Life Out Here" with a series of events. Tractor Supply Company is listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol TSCO.

news.headline: Booming Small Language Model Market: AI Training, Chatbots & More Fuel 17.8% CAGR Growth | Valuates Reports
news.summary: In Bangalore, India, on April 2, 2025, it was reported that the Small Language Model Market has been categorized based on type, with below 5 billion parameters and above 5 billion parameters being the segments. The market is further divided by application into artificial intelligence training, chatbots and virtual assistants, content generation, language translation, and code generation.

news.headline: Dow Jones rises on 'Liberation Day,' Trump stock dives. Tesla jumps despite missed deliveries. Tesla sank due to first quarter deliveries.
news.summary: Today, Tesla's stock took a hit in the market following the release of its first quarter delivery numbers. Despite this, the Dow Jones saw an increase on what was dubbed 'Liberation Day,' while Trump-related stocks experienced a decline. Nvidia shares also dropped, contrasting with Tesla which saw an uptick in its stock performance despite missing its delivery targets. This dynamic movement was covered live by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Lutron launches new website to enhance customer experience; biggest web overhaul in 10 years.
news.summary: In Coopersburg, Pennsylvania, on April 2, 2025, Lutron Electronics, a renowned company specializing in architectural lighting, shading, and control solutions, has announced the launch of the initial stage of their revamped Lutron.com website. This marks a major web redesign for the company in several years.

news.headline: Synchrony provides over $1.5 million in grants to Stamford nonprofits, joins Mayor Simmons to celebrate spring.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, in Stamford, Connecticut, Synchrony (NYSE: SYF), a leading consumer financial services company based in the city, joined forces with Mayor Caroline Simmons, Stamford Downtown, and Stamford Parks Community to host a lively event commemorating spring and fostering community unity.

news.headline: Quantum Gears to display HiTrust r2 Certified Agentic AI Platform at Gartner's event in UAE on April 2, 2025.
news.summary: Quantum Gears, a prominent provider of HiTrust r2 Certified Agentic AI Platform, showcases its dedication to promoting Safe AI in the GCC region. The company shared this commitment in Dubai, UAE, on April 2, 2025. By obtaining the HiTrust r2 certification, Quantum Gears solidifies its position as a trusted entity in the region. This certification highlights the company's adherence to stringent security and compliance standards, ensuring the safety and reliability of its AI solutions. Quantum Gears' participation in the Gartner Security & Risk event underscores its proactive approach to staying updated on the latest industry trends and enhancing its offerings. The HiTrust r2 certification signifies Quantum Gears' emphasis on providing secure and dependable AI solutions, showcasing their commitment to excellence in the field.

news.headline: Smaller companies support Perkins Coie against Trump, top-10 firms abstain from signing brief opposing executive order.
news.summary: Not a single one of the top 10 companies in the nation based on revenue has endorsed a legal document showing support for the law firm currently challenging an executive order. This indicates that the highest-grossing companies have not backed the law firm's stance against the executive order.

news.headline: Kitron updates 2025 financial outlook with strong demand in Defence/Aerospace market, gradual recovery in other segments.
news.summary: Kitron has revised its financial forecast for the year 2025, citing robust demand in the Defence/Aerospace industry. The company is also witnessing a gradual recovery in other market sectors. This update reflects promising growth prospects, with revenue expected to increase significantly in the upcoming period. Kitron's positive outlook is driven by the strong performance in Defence/Aerospace, indicating a prosperous year ahead for the company.

news.headline: Crossroads Treatment Centers is named a USA TODAY Top Workplace for 2025, reflecting their supportive and empowering work culture.
news.summary: Crossroads Treatment Centers in Greenville, South Carolina, on April 2, 2025, has been acknowledged as a Top Workplace in the 2025 USA TODAY awards. This achievement demonstrates Crossroads' commitment to cultivating a positive and empowering work environment. The recognition is a testament to the organization's efforts in creating a workplace where employees feel supported and motivated.

news.headline: Henkel introduces concentrated formulas for laundry brands, reducing plastic waste and promoting sustainability.
news.summary: Henkel, a prominent manufacturer of consumer and industrial brands, is launching concentrated formulas and packaging changes for all® laundry brands, showcasing a dedication to environmental stewardship. This initiative, announced on April 2, 2025, in Stamford, Connecticut, is part of Henkel's commitment to sustainability. The company aims to reduce waste and promote eco-friendly practices by introducing these changes across all of its trusted laundry brands. The move towards concentrated formulas and packaging is a step towards a more sustainable future for Henkel, with the changes set to make a significant impact on reducing the environmental footprint of their products.

news.headline: Trump's tariffs on auto imports may lead to a $26 billion loss in industry profits, wiping out up to 3 million vehicle sales.
news.summary: The article discusses the potential impact of the 25% tariff on imported cars and parts on the U.S. auto industry. Bank of America predicts that this tariff could eliminate up to 3 million vehicle sales, cut automaker profits in half, and disrupt the auto supply chain. The tariffs, set to take effect on April 3, will apply to imported cars, light trucks, and crucial components like engines and transmissions. This move could lead to increased consumer prices and a significant decrease in sales, with a projected loss of 3.2 million units. The article points to the announcement triggering demand destruction, higher prices, and profitability challenges for manufacturers. Further tariffs on parts in May could exacerbate the situation.

news.headline: CHS Inc. reports $7.8 billion in revenues for Q2 FY2025.
news.summary: CHS Inc. announced its financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, reporting revenues of $7.8 billion for the quarter ending on February 28, 2025.

news.headline: Endeavour reports total voting rights in a statement, with 500 million outstanding shares.
European Commission partners with UNICEPTA by PRophet for media analysis across 27 EU states in 32 languages. Stagwell's Comms Tech Unit involved.
news.summary: UNICEPTA, integrated with PRophet's newsroom team, will provide in-depth analysis covering all 27 EU member states in 32 languages. This collaboration between COLOGNE, Germany, BRUSSELS and NEW YORK, was disclosed on April 2, 2025. UNICEPTA by PRophet, operating within Stagwell's renowned Communications Technology Unit, shared this initiative, aiming to offer comprehensive insights across the European Union to a diverse audience.

news.headline: Inventor creates EASY VINYL SIDING INSTALLATION tool for quick and proper nail driving when installing vinyl siding.
news.summary: An inventor from Portsmouth, Rhode Island, spoke about creating a new tool for effectively driving nails during vinyl siding installation. The inventor introduced the EASY VINYL SIDING INSTALLATION tool in response to a need for improved efficiency in the process. This invention aims to streamline the installation process and save time. The specific number of hours or minutes saved was not mentioned in the rephrased text. Additionally, the inventor is based in Pittsburgh and the invention was unveiled on April 2, 2025.

news.headline: TikTok decision could come as soon as today - CNBC'S Faber.

US Payments Forum Spring Market Snapshot discusses AI, biometrics, end of magstripes; facial recognition streamlines transactions, reducing checkout times by up to 90 sec; fraud threats addressed; Mastercard scaling passkeys, aiming for stronger authentication by 2030, enhancing data sharing to counter payment fraud.
news.summary: The U.S. Payments Forum recently held a Spring Member Meeting in San Diego, California, in conjunction with the Identity & Payments Summit, bringing together over 400 stakeholders in various technology sectors. The market snapshot highlighted the impact of biometrics and generative AI on the payments industry, focusing on topics such as the pros and cons of EMV 3-D Secure and plans to phase out magstripes. Facial recognition technology is streamlining transactions globally, reducing checkout times by up to 90 seconds and increasing customer engagement with loyalty programs by two to three times. However, challenges such as accurate demographic representation and protection against fraud need to be addressed as adoption grows. Discussions also centered on secure authentication and the rise of phishing threats fueled by GenAI, with a 4,151% increase in malicious emails noted by the FIDO Alliance. Mastercard outlined plans to enhance data sharing across token and EMV 3-D Secure programs by 2025 to combat payment fraud. The industry is urged to balance innovation with security to ensure continued trust and efficiency in payment methods.

news.headline: Vialytics rethinks road management due to urban-rural spending disparities. Rural counties spend more per capita on infrastructure with lower maintenance costs.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, in Edison, New Jersey, a study conducted by PRNewswire indicated that there are notable discrepancies in road maintenance spending between urban and rural regions. Despite having lower population densities, rural counties were found to allocate more funds per person towards road infrastructure. Interestingly, these areas also experience reduced maintenance costs compared to...

news.headline: CreativeX research finds $47.8 billion potential ad efficiencies with a 10% CQS increase linked to 6.3% CPCV reduction.
news.summary: According to recent studies by CreativeX, enhancing the Creative Quality Score (CQS) could potentially release around $47.8 billion in media savings within the advertising sector. The data shows that for every 10 percentage point rise in CQS, there is a corresponding 6.3% decline in Cost Per Completed View (CPCV). This indicates significant potential for improved efficiencies and cost savings by focusing on CQS enhancements in advertising.

news.headline: EU-Central Asia Summit in Uzbekistan to focus on Economy and Security. Leaders meeting on April 3-4 to strengthen cooperation.
news.summary: In Samarkand, Uzbekistan, leaders from Central Asia and the European Union are convening on April 3-4, 2025, to enhance economic and political collaboration. The summit, initiated by Uzbek President Shavkat Mirziyoyev, marks the first meeting between the five Central Asian nations and the European Union. Key figures like Ursula von der Leyen and Antonio Costa, Presidents of the European Commission and Council respectively, are spearheading discussions aimed at bolstering ties.

news.headline: John Deere and Dovetail Workwear collaborate to empower women in agriculture with special collection.
news.summary: In Moline, Illinois on April 2, 2025, Dovetail Workwear partnered with John Deere to create a line of clothing and equipment tailored for women in agriculture. This joint effort aims to cater to the unique requirements of females in this industry. The collaboration is driven by the vision to empower women in agriculture by providing them with specialized gear.

news.headline: IPX1031 hires Adrienne Salyer as VP, National Accounts to expand sales team. Expert in 1031 services.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, IPX1031, a prominent provider of 1031 Qualified Intermediary services, revealed the appointment of Adrienne Salyer as their new Vice President, National Accounts. This strategic hiring aims to bolster their team as they continue to lead the national market in 1031 exchange services. Salyer's role will involve collaborating with key stakeholders to drive growth and strengthen IPX1031’s presence across the country.

news.headline: Cadence ranked 11th on Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For list in 2025, per the Great Place To Work survey.
news.summary: Cadence, based in San Jose, California, has been named one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2025 by Great Place To Work and Fortune magazine. Cadence has secured the 11th position on this prestigious list.

news.headline: Zuckerberg walked into the west wing, a CNN reporter posts on X.
